proof_groups.proof_strategy: For each witness unfold the return-witness definition, select the second disjunct, introduce parameters, destruct suffix, unfold xizi_dll_suffix in PreH4 and reduce xizi_dll_next_result. In return_wit_1 the nil case yields zero and the cons case contradicts PreH1; in return_wit_2 the nil case contradicts PreH1 and the cons case yields the required next equality. Preserve both xizi_dllseg conjuncts by identity/cancellation.

per_witness_plan.proof_of_xizi_double_link_next_return_wi: right; intros; destruct suffix; nil closes by next-result reduction and spatial identity; cons closes by contradiction between PreH1 and the cons clause of PreH4
right; intros; destruct suffix; nil contradicts PreH1 via the nil clause of PreH4; cons obtains next = head-of-suffix from PreH4; cancel the unchanged segment pair

vc_shape.common_pre_spatial: xizi_dllseg first linklist_pre linklist_node_pre before prefix ** xizi_dllseg next linklist_node_pre linklist_pre last suffix
vc_shape.common_post_spatial: the identical pair of xizi_dllseg resources
vc_shape.pure_split: return_wit_1 proves zero result under next=sentinel; return_wit_2 proves next result under next<>sentinel

proof_groups.space_plan: Frame/cancel both xizi_dllseg predicates unchanged; no segment decomposition is required.
proof_groups.pure_plan: Destruct suffix, unfold the suffix relation and result function, and use exactly PreH1 plus the appropriate clause of PreH4.

premise_discharge.definitions: xizi_dll_suffix and xizi_dll_next_result reduce on suffix
premise_discharge.empty_nonempty_branch_facts: from PreH1 and the nil/cons clauses of PreH4
premise_discharge.spatial: identity of the two xizi_dllseg resources

proof_groups.grouping_reason: Both target witnesses share a single suffix-destructor and framing proof pattern, require no helper declarations, and fit within max_witnesses_per_group=12.
